segments.text: Good evening, madam. I am Dr. Parita speaking from Tata AIA Life Insurance.
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Which language are you comfortable in? Gujarati, Hindi, or English?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: To proceed with the policy you have taken, this is a telephonic medical verification call, where I will ask you a few verification and health-related questions.
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: If we continue this call, it will take about 10 to 12 minutes.
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Okay. For your information, I want to tell you that this call is recorded for the company's audit and quality purposes.
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Whatever answers you give regarding your health and lifestyle, they become a part of your insurance application, and if your proposal is accepted, it becomes a legal contract between you and Tata. If your answers are incorrect or incomplete, it will affect your contract, and there's a chance your claim might be rejected or policy canceled.
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: That's why I request you to give all correct answers, okay?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: For verification, we have some details. I am looking at your application. Your full name is given as Parulben Patel. Is that correct?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Can you tell me your date of birth as per the application?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: 10th grade. What do you do? Job, business, or housewife?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Housewife, and I have a shop.
segments.speaker: customer

segments.text: Housewife and I have a shop.
segments.speaker: customer

segments.text: Okay, is it your own shop? Do you run the shop yourself?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Yes, it's my own. My husband and I run it. I'm there sometimes, he's there more.
segments.speaker: customer

segments.text: Okay, so should I write both housewife and business?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Okay. For ID proof verification, please tell me the last four digits of your Aadhaar card.
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Last four digits of Aadhaar card. Yes.
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: 8077. From which city are you speaking now?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Anand. Anand, Aatish, I am speaking from there.
segments.speaker: customer

segments.text: Anand, Gujarat. Okay, madam. Now I will ask some questions related to your health and lifestyle, okay?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: In the last two years, have you consumed any substances like tobacco or cigarettes?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Have you ever had any addiction that a doctor told you to quit or reduce, such as tobacco, alcohol, etc.?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Have you ever taken drugs or narcotics not prescribed by a doctor?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: 68 kg. Okay. Now I will name some diseases. If you had that disease before or have it now, say yes. If not, you can say no after hearing the full question.
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Do you have any problems like high blood pressure or low blood pressure (fluctuation in BP), high cholesterol, triglycerides, or lipids (high fat levels)?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Ever had chest pain, heaviness, or anxiety, heart murmur (abnormal heart sound), or a heart attack?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Any heart-related disease, heart valve problems, congenital heart disease, increased heart rate, rheumatic fever, or cough due to heart issues?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Cancer or pre-cancerous conditions like leukemia (blood cancer), swollen lymph nodes, skin lesions, or dysplasia?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Leukoplakia (white patches in mouth), sarcoma (bone cancer), or any type of tumor, polyp, nodule, cyst, lump, or mass in the body?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Okay. Diabetes (madhumeh), high blood sugar in blood or urine, or thyroid problems like hyperthyroidism or hypothyroidism, or any other thyroid-related disease?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Okay. Respiratory diseases like asthma, bronchitis, sinus problems, tuberculosis, pneumonia, or long-term cough?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Sleep apnea (difficulty breathing during sleep), emphysema, COPD (lung disease), or any other respiratory disease?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Hepatitis, hepatitis carrier, fatty liver, liver cirrhosis, jaundice, or liver disease due to alcohol consumption, or any other liver-related diseases?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Blood-related problems like anemia (blood deficiency), thalassemia (blood not forming), systemic lupus erythematosus, or any other blood-related disease or autoimmune disease?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Anxiety, depression, seizures, paralysis, weakness in limbs, or stroke?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Transient ischemic attack (blood not reaching brain nerves), multiple sclerosis, Parkinson's (tremors), dementia, Alzheimer's (forgetfulness), or any other neurological, nervous, or mental health problems?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Stomach-related diseases like gastritis (stomach inflammation), gastroesophageal reflux disease (vomiting, nausea, burning in throat), stomach ulcer, or frequent indigestion?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Stomach and intestinal diseases like irritable bowel syndrome (intestinal problems), ulcerative colitis, atrophic gastritis, or Crohn's disease (intestinal inflammation)?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Ulcerative colitis (ulcers in intestines), or any other pancreatic, stomach, or intestinal diseases?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Kidney stones, gallstones, kidney or urine infection, or blood or protein in urine?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Long-term kidney disease, kidney failure, piles (hemorrhoids), fistula (bhagandar), or any other kidney, urinary, or reproductive system disease?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Problems like back pain, joint pain (arthritis), gout, or slipped disc (vertebrae shifting), muscular dystrophy, or deformity?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Any physical disability or other disease related to muscles, bones, joints, spine, or spinal cord?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Okay. Eye-related problems like not seeing, low vision, double vision, glaucoma, cataracts, or need for glasses?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Okay. Ear-related problems like not hearing, low hearing, difficulty speaking, abnormal voice, difficulty eating, pus from ear, or other ear/throat related diseases?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Besides this, is there anything else about your health or habits that you think is necessary to mention for the policy?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Okay. Besides everything you've told me, in the last five years, any medical condition where you had to undergo tests and the reports were not normal, or tests are still pending, like X-ray, sonography, MRI, PET scan, or CT scan?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Or other reports like biopsy (tissue test), electrocardiogram, treadmill test, endoscopy, colonoscopy, mammography, Pap smear, blood, or urine tests?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Any treatment or medication that lasted more than two weeks and a specialist doctor advised you for treatment, consultation, or other reports?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Any instance where you couldn't go to work for more than two weeks or had to rest due to illness?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Any congenital disease like cerebral palsy, Down syndrome, or poliomyelitis?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Okay, I apologize for asking this question, but it's necessary due to protocol, so I'm asking.
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Have you or your spouse ever been advised by a doctor for any test or treatment, or counseled for HIV AIDS?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Have you or your spouse ever been advised by a doctor for any test or treatment, or counseled for HIV AIDS?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Besides everything you've told me, have you ever experienced significant weight fluctuations or other symptoms for which you are seeing a doctor, or is there any treatment or surgery pending that you wish to undergo?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Okay. What is the date of your last menstrual period?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Is the period still ongoing or has it stopped?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Okay, so tell me the date of your last menstrual period.
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: 25/2. Okay, so in March, you didn't have a period?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: It came but then it stopped.
segments.speaker: customer

segments.text: Yes, so you have to tell the very last date, madam.
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: So your period has become irregular due to menopause?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Yes, it happens now, but sometimes it comes, sometimes it doesn't. That's the problem.
segments.speaker: customer

segments.text: No, I am just asking for the last date. When was your last menstrual period, tell me the date and month.
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Any problems during pregnancy like gestational diabetes, pre-eclampsia, or other complications?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Okay. Have you ever consulted a doctor for a lump in the breast area or a breast disease, irregular periods, pain during periods, or an abnormal Pap smear?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Or for breast or gynecological conditions like vaginal disorders, or problems related to the uterus, ovary, or fallopian tube?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Okay, okay. So, now due to menopause, it's a bit irregular, sometimes less, sometimes more, right?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Okay. Do you declare and agree that all the answers you have given to my questions are true and complete, and your answers will be recorded in the policy records, right?
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Thank you for your time and cooperation. Have a good day.
segments.speaker: doctor

segments.text: Thank you, madam. Have a good day.
segments.speaker: doctor
